declaredLane: Unity MCP editor-session safety, forward rule capture, and UI authoring verification guard
observedMutationClass: codex guardrail owner update, Unity UI authoring checkpoint update, Unity MCP editmode helper safe default, workflow/reference alignment, and recurrence closeout update
acceptanceEvidenceClass: PowerShell syntax check for the Unity MCP helper plus rules:sync-closeout and docs lint; repo-wide rules lint still depends on unrelated runtime hotspot cleanup
rootCause: The Unity MCP EditMode helper auto-stopped Play Mode by default. In an open editor session, especially when queued scene changes or user-owned scene inspection are present, that auxiliary verification path can surface an unexpected scene dirty/save prompt. The repo guardrails also did not explicitly require preserve-play-mode/block-by-default behavior or forbid parallel Unity shared-resource helpers for this case.
prevention: Default `Invoke-UnityMcpEditModeTests.ps1` to preserve the current editor session unless `-AllowPlayModeStop` is explicit, record the no-parallel Unity shared-resource rule in ops.codex-coding-guardrails, and add the same preserve-play-mode expectation to the Unity UI authoring and Unity MCP execution references. Preserve the existing hard-fail checks for active plan artifact owner shape drift, active plan concrete artifact owner collision, and progress evidence overload.
